reasoning:
The question asks if the market close price of IEX on 2026-03-22 will be higher than on 2026-03-15. This is a one-week ahead stock price forecast. Daily stock moves are nearly a random walk with a very slight upward drift reflecting long-term equity risk premium (approx 52-54% chance of a positive week historically). An analysis of historical prices for IEX around this time of year gives a marginal upward signal (a simple linear trend yields roughly 55% chance, though the sample from Yahoo Finance tool is limited to recent months and prior-year points). 
I searched for any scheduled mechanistic occurrences (like an ex-dividend date), but the next dividends usually go ex-date in Jan, Apr, Jul, Oct. Furthermore, earnings reports are clustered around Feb and April/May, meaning there aren't scheduled earnings releases strictly between March 15 and March 22 that might drastically impact volatility one way or another. Institutional moves reported in 13Fs are routine trailing news, and there's no major forward-looking M&A catalyst spotted. Consequently, without a known directional catalyst or dividend mechanical drop, the probability should sit near the statistical baseline of a random 1-week walk of a healthy-paying industrial stock, which is slightly above 0.5. I will estimate the probability of the price increasing over this 1-week period at 53%, reflecting the slight base-rate upward drift of equities over any given week in the absence of a known negative catalyst.
